case_title: MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC. VS. MITCHCO, INC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: STIPULATION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT (TRANSACTION ID # 100009144) FILED BY MITCHCO, INC. DBA RUDY ART GLASS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MITCHCO, INC., DBA RUDY ART GLASS'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. MITCHCO, INC. HAS MET ITS BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING A VALID AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE EXISTS AND ENCOMPASSES THE CLAIMS PRESENTED IN PLAINTIFF MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT INC., DBA MARTIN BUILDING COMPANY'S COMPLAINT. THIS CASE IS ORDERED STAYED PENDING OUTCOME OF THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Defendant Mitchco, Inc.S Notice Of Hearing And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceeding
Off Calendar Feb-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Dana Andreoli of the law offices of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (415.421.3400) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Paul J. Steiner] of the law offices of Paul J. Steiner (415.981.6100) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MITCHCO, INC., dba Rudy Art Glass's PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS is GRANTED. Mitchco, Inc. has met its burden of establishing a valid agreement to arbitrate exists and encompasses the claims presented in plaintiff Martin McNerney Development Inc., dba Martin Building Company's complaint. The order confirmation lays out specifications for the glass and installation specifications and the claims all arise out of the manufacture and installation of the glass. The arbitration agreement is not unconscionable because plaintiff has not shown procedural unconscionability. Though the arbitration agreement was presented on a form, it was not offered on a take-it-or-leave-it basis, as evidenced by the last page of the order confirmation which allowed acceptance without modification, acceptance with modification, or rejection pending further discussion. California has a strong policy in favor of arbitrating disputes and plaintiff has not shown overriding policy concerns warranting a denial of defendant's petition. This case is ordered stayed pending outcome of the arbitration proceedings. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
